title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Claim Defense
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for security, privacy, reliability, and layered quality evidence and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for trust and quality assurance case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
Trace purpose, collection, consent or authority, use, sharing, retention, access, correction, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Assign pure behavior, boundary, integration, interface, end-to-end, accessibility, security, and recovery claims to appropriate tests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Quality Nonclaim symptom in trust and quality assurance case during this quiz case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

hints: For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.

stimulus.title: Quality Nonclaim failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

options.text: Accept the Quality Nonclaim result because visible trust and quality assurance case output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Quality Nonclaim.

For diagnose the hidden failure, apply Quality Nonclaim without its stated preconditions, omit the trust and quality assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Quality Nonclaim for trust and quality assurance case.
Run the Quality Nonclaim boundary case for trust and quality assurance case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Threat Abuse Case
steps.instruction:
Order the Threat Abuse Case evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Threat Abuse Case.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Threat Abuse Case assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Quality Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: Retrieving Quality Nonclaim through the concrete trust and quality assurance case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

hints: For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Quality Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Quality Nonclaim, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.
